summary.intent: Add an e2e test covering GitHub issue #729 (user messages from external API/non-web clients not appearing in chat) and plan a backend+frontend fix.
summary.outcome: Created a new e2e spec file with 3 tests marked test.fixme() and produced a detailed fix plan spanning backend Rust, frontend JS, and iOS Swift. A commit was staged and pushed with a PR created.

repo: The web UI uses optimistic local rendering for user messages — it adds the message to DOM before calling chat.send, so the missing broadcast only affects non-web clients (GraphQL, mobile, channel integrations).
Channel messages already broadcast via a channel_user event dispatched in crates/gateway/src/channel_events/dispatch.rs:35 — the user_message fix should follow the same pattern.
WebSocket chat event handlers live in crates/web/src/assets/js/websocket.js in a chatHandlers dispatch map around line 1057.
E2e tests use a test.fixme() convention to skip tests for unimplemented features until the fix lands.
The project uses Biome for JS linting.

code.finding: User message is persisted here but no WebSocket broadcast event is emitted afterward — root cause of issue #729.
chatHandlers dispatch map — a user_message handler needs to be added here.
channel_user event broadcast — reference pattern for the new user_message broadcast.

workflow: Used test.fixme() to commit failing/skipped tests ahead of the fix, keeping CI green while tracking the work.
Created a beads issue to track the fix work separately from the e2e test PR.
Ran Biome lint on the new test file before committing to catch issues early.

friction: Needed multiple rounds of grep/read to trace the full send_impl flow and confirm exactly where persistence happens vs where broadcast is missing.
Had to cross-reference three separate layers (backend Rust, frontend JS, iOS Swift) to produce a complete fix plan.
open_items: Implement the actual fix: broadcast user_message event in crates/chat/src/service/chat_impl/send.rs after persistence.
Implement handleChatUserMessage in crates/web/src/assets/js/websocket.js following the channel_user pattern, including dedup logic via client seq.
Add case userMessage = "user_message" to ChatEventState enum in apps/ios/Sources/Networking/ChatEventTypes.swift.
Remove test.fixme() from all 3 e2e tests once the fix is implemented and merged.
